html:
Full lateral trajectory
All camera positions are shown with rightward arrows indicating pure lateral translation.
⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: five identical perspective camera frustums each positioned sequentially along a horizontal line
solid black circle (camera center) at the base of each frustum
thin black lines forming the frustum shape for each camera
six thick red horizontal arrows pointing to the right, connecting one camera to the next

interactions.label: Lateral displacement
interactions.teaches: Slides the active camera laterally, showing how the camera's center shifts parallel to the image plane with no rotation or forward motion

interactions.label: Show path history
interactions.teaches: Toggles whether all camera positions (trail) are displayed, illustrating the full translation or just the current position

demo_steps.title: Camera at initial position
demo_steps.narration: Here is a single camera at rest, facing directly ahead. The black frustum represents the field of view, and the black dot its center. There is no motion yet.

demo_steps.title: Begin lateral translation
demo_steps.narration: Now, slide the camera laterally to the right. Only its horizontal position changes; there is no rotation or forward motion. The frustum and center move together.

demo_steps.title: Full lateral trajectory
demo_steps.narration: Let's reveal the entire motion path. All intermediate camera positions appear, connected by red arrows showing the direction of translation. This sequence represents pure lateral camera motion, like looking out the side window of a moving vehicle.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Front orthographic view, looking straight at the row of camera centers and frustums from above. All positions and arrows are clearly visible horizontally across the frame.
interactionPlan.notes: Each camera and frustum should be drawn using lines for geometric clarity. Red arrows are rendered as thick, semi-transparent arrows between neighboring camera centers, always horizontal and rightward. Animate lateralDisplacement as a smooth translation along the X-axis; trail positions are simply frozen copies at each integer X step. No 3D parallax needed for this conceptual view.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Red arrows in the generated figure are significantly shorter and chunkier compared to the long, thin arrows in the source.
Red arrows in the generated figure do not extend the full distance between the cameras as in the source figure.
Spacing between cameras is slightly less even in the generated figure compared to the perfectly uniform spacing in the source.
Arrowheads are too large and visually dominate the graphic more than in the source.
UI controls (slider, buttons) are present in the generated version but not in the original figure.
evaluation.notes: The core concept and camera/frustum arrangement is clear and controls work well, but the arrows are visually off in scale and style and some spacing details do not match.
action_items: Redesign the red arrows to be much longer and thinner so they visually link the cameras in the same way as the source.
Reduce the size of arrowheads so they more closely resemble the source figure's proportions.
Ensure perfectly even camera spacing and that all arrows consistently span between cameras.
Optionally provide a display mode without UI controls to closely match the source's static appearance.
Tweak camera geometry line widths for a crisper, textbook-like render.

feedback.rationale: The conceptual decomposition is sound but the generator's output has visual inaccuracies (arrow scale, alignment, style, and UI controls) that are all addressable through rendering and implementation refinement.

Optionally provide a display mode without UI controls to closely match the source's static appearance.
Tweak camera geometry line widths for a crisper, textbook-like render.

attempts.html:
Full lateral trajectory
All camera positions are shown with rightward arrows indicating pure lateral translation.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Generated camera symbols are very close but slightly more squat than the reference (minor vertical scaling mismatch).
Arrowheads in the generated figure are slightly more stubby and shorter than in the reference.
UI panel and controls are present in the rendered output but not part of the original flat diagram.
Rendered arrows start and end positions differ slightly relative to camera positions compared to the source.
evaluation.notes: Camera and arrow geometry are very close to the source, with functional and instructive interactivity and excellent UI/label clarity; minor geometric/arrows axis ratio differences present.
action_items: Adjust the aspect ratio and vertical scaling of the camera symbol to better match the tall, narrow proportions of the reference.
Refine arrowhead length and sharpness to more closely match the source figure's longer arrowheads.
Slightly adjust horizontal spacing and vertical alignment of cameras and arrows for perfect compositional match.
Consider optionally hiding the UI panel for a 'figure-only' export if needed for textbook embedding.

feedback.rationale: All failure modes are purely visual refinements without concept errors or missing elements; high scores and action items focus on minor geometric and aesthetic tweaks, so further iteration is unnecessary.
action_items: Adjust the aspect ratio and vertical scaling of the camera symbol to better match the tall, narrow proportions of the reference.
Refine arrowhead length and sharpness to more closely match the source figure's longer arrowheads.
Slightly adjust horizontal spacing and vertical alignment of cameras and arrows for perfect compositional match.
Consider optionally hiding the UI panel for a 'figure-only' export if needed for textbook embedding.
